steps.text: The cell membrane controls what enters and leaves the cell.
Cytoplasm is the jelly-like fluid where organelles float.
The nucleus holds DNA and controls cell activity.
Mitochondria are the powerhouse - they make ATP energy.
The endoplasmic reticulum transports materials through the cell.
The Golgi apparatus packages and ships proteins.
Ribosomes build proteins from amino acids.
Each organelle has a specific job - together they keep the cell alive and working.
